Keystone commensal involved in carbohydrate fermentation and immune modulation, supporting gut barrier integrity and microbial balance.
Beneficial fermenters that produce short-chain fatty acids, support intestinal barrier function and inhibit pathogen colonization.
Normal gut commensals involved in metabolic activity and microbial competition; elevated levels may reflect dysbiosis or inflammation.
Common facultative commensals that participate in early gut colonization and nutrient metabolism within the microbiome.
Lactic acid-producing commensals that maintain acidic gut conditions, inhibit pathogens and support mucosal immunity.
Facultative anaerobes involved in nutrient metabolism; overrepresentation may indicate microbial imbalance or inflammatory conditions.
Mucin-degrading bacterium that supports mucus layer turnover, gut barrier integrity and metabolic health.
Major butyrate-producing commensal that supports gut barrier integrity and anti-inflammatory immune regulation.
Fiber-fermenting bacteria producing butyrate that support intestinal barrier health and microbial stability.
